target: data/wins.json WIN-068 — two targeted paragraph-scope replacements resolving ISS-1490 (c12 H1 moderate: scope leak in detail_verdict_text P2 and parallel detail_evidence FSF-retraction passage). Both passages currently discuss the dome's 2026 eclipse FSF formula and our retraction of the "empirical fitting" framing, both of which are WIN-010/025 scope (where FSF actually enters the predicted signal) and not the 39-eclipse Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble evaluated in WIN-068 (where FSF is a spectator). Each replacement compresses the scope-leaky paragraph to a cross-reference sentence pointing to WIN-010, preserves the in-scope "FSF is a spectator" framing, and retains mention of the retraction and Pearson-r check so readers who only see WIN-068 do not lose the retraction signal. No edits to detail_claim, finding, claim, detail_extra, code_analysis, tldr_evidence, or tldr_verdict.
patch_instructions: Two atomic substring replacements on WIN-068 in data/wins.json: PATCH-A on detail_verdict_text (compresses P2), PATCH-B on detail_evidence (compresses the parallel FSF-retraction passage inside ¶3). Both BEFORE strings verified unique globally (occurrence_count = 1 across every stringly field of every WIN) on 2026-04-23. Apply both; verify afterward that (a) the surviving WIN-068 detail_verdict_text still contains the phrase "FSF does not enter the 1991–2016 data at all" (retained in the unmodified P3, which is the in-scope FSF framing), (b) the surviving WIN-068 detail_evidence still contains "FSF is a spectator" (retained in the modified PATCH-B text), (c) WIN-010 and WIN-025 retain their own FSF-retraction and direct-recomputation text unchanged (not touched by this EXP — verified), and (d) both #win010 and #win025 anchors resolve (they are existing WIN IDs). Projected dry-run deltas: detail_verdict_text 4614 → 4378 (delta −236); detail_evidence 3764 → 3694 (delta −70). No TLDR, finding, detail_claim, claim, detail_extra, or code_analysis edits.

patches.target: data/wins.json → WIN-068 → detail_verdict_text → paragraph 2 (the "Credit where it is due on the scaling itself" paragraph)
patches.rationale: P2 currently spends 755 characters explaining that the dome's 2026 eclipse FSF scaling factor really is geometric, direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, and our prior "empirical fitting" framing was quantitatively wrong and has been retracted. Every sentence here is about the 2026 forward projection and the FSF=H(r)/r check — neither of which enters the 39-eclipse Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble that WIN-068 actually evaluates. The c12 curmudgeon correctly flags this as scope leak: readers looking for why the 39-eclipse ensemble does not discriminate the dome from standard Sq ionospheric physics are forced through an unrelated retraction about a 2026 single-eclipse scaling factor. The AFTER text replaces P2 with a single cross-reference sentence that (a) preserves the retraction signal (mentions the 2026 formula, the Pearson-r check, and the retraction explicitly so a reader who only reads WIN-068 still knows we retracted that earlier framing), (b) defers the full discussion to WIN-010 and WIN-025 where FSF actually enters the predicted signal, and (c) adds a new in-scope clarifier: "FSF is not invoked anywhere in the 39-eclipse Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble evaluated in this WIN." This snaps P3's existing opening sentence ("Being right about the scaling law between stations is not the same as predicting the signal") into cleaner logical position because P3 now begins immediately after a sentence that says FSF is not invoked here, rather than after a defense of FSF's geometric legitimacy.
patches.before: Credit where it is due on the scaling itself: the dome's 2026 eclipse formula (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) uses a field-strength factor that genuinely equals H(r)/r normalized to Boulder. Solving the dome's own V12 coordinate equation r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) for each station's latitude and computing (H(r)/r)/(H(rBOU)/rBOU) reproduces the dome's nine published FSF values (0.642–2.075) to better than 0.1% at seven of nine stations and within 1.8% at the worst (Pearson r = 0.9999 between predicted and claimed). Our prior framing of FSF as "empirical fitting, not dome-geometric derivation" was quantitatively wrong and has been retracted in the WIN-010/025/068 cluster; the dome's FSF really is dome geometry.
patches.after: On the FSF scaling law itself — the 2026 eclipse formula (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF), the direct-recomputation check against r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) that reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, and our retraction of the prior "empirical fitting" framing — see WIN-010 and WIN-025, where FSF actually enters the predicted signal. FSF is not invoked anywhere in the 39-eclipse Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble evaluated in this WIN.
patches.dry_run: Node replace() dry-run executed 2026-04-23; before-string length 755, after-string length 519, delta −236; new detail_verdict_text length 4378 (from 4614).

patches.target: data/wins.json → WIN-068 → detail_evidence → scope-leaky FSF-retraction passage inside paragraph 3 (from " For completeness: the dome's FSF scaling factor" through "...not from dome geometry.")
patches.rationale: detail_evidence ¶3 ends with a ~515-character parallel to the detail_verdict_text P2 scope leak: the same FSF=H(r)/r direct-recomputation check, the same Pearson r = 0.9999, and the same retraction of the "empirical fitting" framing. These belong in WIN-010 (where FSF actually enters the predicted signal) and in WIN-025 (2024 9-station where the same FSF values apply), not here. The AFTER text preserves the retraction signal in a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010, then keeps the in-scope punchline intact: "For the 39-eclipse ensemble evaluated here, FSF is a spectator: the ensemble statistical method derives its expected signal amplitude from the 1991–2016 dataset itself, not from dome geometry." This reads cleanly as the final sentence of ¶3 and requires no restructuring of the rest of the paragraph.
patches.before: For completeness: the dome's FSF scaling factor (relevant to the WIN-010 2026 forward projection) is a legitimate geometric derivation — direct recomputation reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999, so FSF = H(r)/r is real and we retract our prior "empirical fitting" framing. That concession is germane to WIN-010, not WIN-068. For WIN-068, FSF is a spectator: the ensemble statistical method derives its expected signal amplitude from the 39-eclipse dataset itself, not from dome geometry.
patches.after: The dome's FSF scaling factor, the direct-recomputation check (Pearson r = 0.9999), and our retraction of the prior "empirical fitting" framing are germane to the WIN-010 2026 forward projection — see WIN-010 for that discussion. For the 39-eclipse ensemble evaluated here, FSF is a spectator: the ensemble statistical method derives its expected signal amplitude from the 1991–2016 dataset itself, not from dome geometry.
patches.dry_run: Node replace() dry-run executed 2026-04-23; before-string length 515, after-string length 445, delta −70; new detail_evidence length 3694 (from 3764).

holes_addressed.description: detail_verdict_text P2 ("Credit where it is due on the scaling itself...") discusses the dome's 2026 eclipse FSF formula — a WIN-010/025 topic, not a WIN-068 topic. Parallel passage in detail_evidence (¶3 tail, "For completeness: the dome's FSF scaling factor...") has the same scope bleed. The curmudgeon recommended compressing both to cross-reference sentences pointing to WIN-010/025 and updating the evidence passage similarly.
holes_addressed.resolved_by: PATCH-A and PATCH-B jointly. P2 in detail_verdict_text is replaced with a single cross-reference sentence that defers to WIN-010 and WIN-025 and adds the in-scope clarifier that FSF is not invoked in the 39-eclipse ensemble. The parallel passage in detail_evidence ¶3 is replaced with a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010, followed by the retained in-scope punchline ("For the 39-eclipse ensemble evaluated here, FSF is a spectator..."). Together the patches reclaim roughly 306 characters of WIN-068 prose and remove the cross-WIN confusion.

scope_preservation_checks: P3 of detail_verdict_text ("Being right about the scaling law between stations...") is unchanged and continues to carry the in-scope framing that FSF does not enter the 1991-2016 data (verified: newDVT.includes("FSF does not enter the 1991–2016 data at all") === true).

Paragraph 4 (Steelman Tier-1), Paragraph 5 (cross-reference to WIN-010 Section 1.8 coordinate critique), Paragraph 6 (WIN-010/025/068 triple-count inflation), and Paragraph 7 (post-hoc test) are unchanged.

detail_evidence ¶1 (Kim & Chang underlying data), ¶2 (refraction-indeterminacy internal-consistency note), and the leading ~⅔ of ¶3 (Chapman 1933 mechanism, Sq conductivity, Yamazaki & Maute 2021 link, ionospheric-parameter-source argument) are all unchanged.

WIN-010 and WIN-025 detail_* fields are NOT touched by this EXP. The FSF retraction and direct-recomputation text in those WINs remains the authoritative discussion site (verified: grep for "Pearson r = 0.9999" and "empirical fitting" in WIN-010 confirms the retraction text is present and owned by WIN-010).

anticipated_objections.objection: By removing the Pearson r = 0.9999 number and the V12 r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) equation from WIN-068 detail_verdict_text, readers of WIN-068 alone will not know how we verified the FSF derivation. The retraction is less credible without the arithmetic.
anticipated_objections.response: PATCH-A retains the critical quantitative anchors in condensed form — "the direct-recomputation check against r·tan(θ) = 8537·exp(−r/8619) that reproduces the nine published FSF values at Pearson r = 0.9999" appears verbatim in the AFTER sentence. Readers who follow the cross-reference to WIN-010 get the full per-station breakdown; readers who do not still see the key claim and the anchor equation. PATCH-B similarly retains "Pearson r = 0.9999" in the one-sentence cross-reference. The retraction signal is preserved at both sites, just proportioned to scope.

anticipated_objections.objection: Could a dome defender claim this compression is a rhetorical retreat — that we removed the concession because it was embarrassing to us, not because of scope?
anticipated_objections.response: No: both the AFTER sentences state the retraction explicitly ("our retraction of the prior 'empirical fitting' framing" in PATCH-A; "our retraction of the prior 'empirical fitting' framing" in PATCH-B). The retraction is preserved verbatim as self-correction; only the detailed arithmetic is deferred to WIN-010 where it materially affects the predicted signal. A neutral reader can still see that we made a retraction and where to read more. The spring-cleaning is scope-motivated — and demonstrable by the symmetric fact that the retained WIN-068 prose now focuses exclusively on the 39-eclipse ensemble (Chapman 1933, Kim & Chang 2018, Yamazaki & Maute 2021, ionospheric Sq dynamo), which is actually what WIN-068 is about.

anticipated_objections.objection: Considering the other side of the argument (per user preference): could a reader argue the FSF discussion, even if about WIN-010/025, is legitimately relevant here because all three WINs cite the same eclipse phenomenon and the critique should handle them as a cluster?
anticipated_objections.response: Partially steelmanned, partially rejected. The cluster framing IS retained — paragraph 6 of detail_verdict_text (unchanged) explicitly says: "WIN-010 (BOU 2017), WIN-025 (2024 9-station), and WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble 1991-2016) all cite observations explained by the same ionospheric Sq mechanism at three different aggregation levels." The cluster treatment survives. What is removed is the WIN-010-specific FSF mechanics. A reader coming to WIN-068 to understand the 39-eclipse ensemble critique now reads a focused argument about what that ensemble does and does not establish; a reader coming to the cluster framing still sees the three WINs treated together. The compression does not weaken the anti-duplication argument; if anything it sharpens the boundary between "same phenomenon counted three times" (which is correct and preserved) and "FSF mechanics apply to all three" (which is false — FSF does not enter the 39-eclipse ensemble at all).

kernel_of_truth: The scope-leaky content (FSF is geometrically real, Pearson r = 0.9999, retracting "empirical fitting" framing) is not wrong — it is correct and materially important in WIN-010/025. The c12 curmudgeon's finding is structural, not factual: the retraction is being spoken in the wrong courtroom. WIN-068 evaluates a 39-eclipse statistical ensemble where FSF is never invoked; the retraction belongs in the WIN where FSF is actually used.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_the_dome: The dome does not benefit from this compression. The retraction signal is still present in WIN-068 (condensed to one cross-reference sentence per field) AND present in full at WIN-010 and WIN-025 where it has operational weight. The dome's eclipse score inflation (same phenomenon counted as three WINs) is preserved unchanged in paragraph 6. If anything, the dome-side case weakens slightly after the compression because the WIN-068 argument is now undiluted by a WIN-010-specific concession — the 39-eclipse Chapman-Sq explanation stands alone.
cross_references: EXP-235 (integrated prior) rewrote WIN-010/025/068 for the FSF retraction; that EXP introduced the cluster framing and the retraction language. EXP-241 does NOT unwind the retraction — it relocates the full retraction discussion to WIN-010 where FSF operationally matters, leaving a cross-reference in WIN-068 where FSF is a spectator.
ISS-1486 (WIN-025 architectural disjoint) handled separately by EXP-239 (already-resolved). Not affected by this EXP.
ISS-1487+1488 (WIN-069 circuity citation retraction) handled separately by EXP-240 (prior run). Not affected by this EXP.

ISS-1489 (WIN-061 predictions.json structured field vs prose contradiction) is a separate assigned-analyst issue targeting predictions.json, not wins.json; being addressed in a companion EXP from this run.
unaddressed_holes_note: P3 of detail_verdict_text ("Being right about the scaling law between stations...") contains some remaining 2026-projection detail (Boulder anchor −10.9 nT adoption, in-sample evaluation against 2017 BOU) that could be argued also leans WIN-010 direction. The c12 curmudgeon did NOT flag P3 — only P2 — so we do not scope-creep. If a future curmudgeon pass flags P3, the decider can queue a separate issue. This EXP closes ISS-1490 as scoped; no other c12 holes touched.
notes_for_decider: Two atomic substring replacements on WIN-068. Both BEFORE strings verified unique across all WINs and all stringly fields (occurrence_count = 1, global) on 2026-04-23 at dry-run time. PATCH-A on detail_verdict_text: delta −236, new length 4378. PATCH-B on detail_evidence: delta −70, new length 3694. After integration: (a) run tests; (b) verify both anchors (#win010 and #win025) resolve in the built HTML (grep-check against docs/index.html after build); (c) confirm retraction signal still present in WIN-068 by substring-checking for "retraction of the prior" in both fields; (d) confirm the UTF-8 en-dashes (−, –) and ampersand entities (&) are preserved. UTF-8 characters in the AFTER text: en-dash (−) in "δ" etc., em-dash (—) separators, & inside "Kim & Chang". The word "FSF" appears 1× in new DVT P2 (down from 7× in old P2) and 1× in new DE passage (down from 4×).
deeper_analysis:
This is a second-order self-correction: EXP-235 (integrated prior to this run) correctly added the FSF retraction to WIN-068, but treated all three eclipse WINs (010, 025, 068) uniformly, which caused the retraction language to appear identically across the cluster even though FSF only operationally enters WIN-010 (2026 forward projection, 9 stations) and WIN-025 (2024 9-station, same FSF values). WIN-068 evaluates the Kim & Chang 1991–2016 39-eclipse ensemble, where FSF is structurally absent — the ensemble statistical method derives amplitudes from the dataset itself. By uniformly cloning the retraction across the cluster, EXP-235 over-applied a correction that belonged to WIN-010/025. EXP-241 corrects the over-application without rescinding the correction: the retraction remains visible in WIN-068 as a cross-reference sentence, and its full substance remains at WIN-010/025 where it matters.

Considering the other side (per user preference): a reasonable counter is that cross-references split the reading experience and make the review harder to follow than a self-contained WIN-068 entry. Response: (1) the review already uses extensive cross-referencing (WIN-010 ↔ WIN-025 ↔ WIN-068 ↔ Section 1.8 ↔ Timestamp Error tab) as a navigational backbone; adding two more anchor links is on-pattern, not friction. (2) The compressed WIN-068 text is actually easier to read because it has one focused argument (the 39-eclipse ensemble is explained by Chapman-Sq and does not invoke FSF) rather than two tangled arguments (Chapman-Sq + FSF defense + FSF retraction). (3) The retraction signal is preserved at both sites — nobody misses it, whether they read WIN-068 alone or the cluster as a whole.

On the architectural principle: the rule surfaced here is "every WIN's prose should be about that WIN." When a claim applies to multiple WINs in a cluster, the right move is to put the full discussion at the WIN where the claim operationally enters (the "owner"), and put cross-reference pointers at the other WINs. WIN-010 owns FSF (it enters the predicted signal). WIN-025 also owns FSF partially (same 9-station setup, same FSF values). WIN-068 is a spectator. The EXP-241 compression respects that ownership structure.
tldr: Two targeted paragraph-scope replacements on WIN-068 (in detail_verdict_text P2 and in the parallel FSF-retraction passage of detail_evidence ¶3), jointly resolving ISS-1490 (c12 H1 moderate: scope leak into WIN-010/025 territory). Both BEFORE strings verified unique globally (occurrence_count=1 across all fields of all WINs). The retraction signal is preserved in both patched locations as a one-sentence cross-reference to WIN-010/025; the in-scope framing (FSF is a spectator in the 39-eclipse ensemble) is retained. Projected deltas: detail_verdict_text −236 chars, detail_evidence −70 chars. No TLDR, finding, detail_claim, or code_analysis edits.
